comments.text: Your example does not work. This forbids a family of hypergraphs (if you merely forbid the one that corresponds to 3-APs, then taking all edges containing $1$ avoids this). There are true single hypergraphs which avoid this. The original example was given by Frankl and Furedi, see https://arxiv.org/pdf/1906.06657 for further discussion. (The site has been updated to address this comment.)

For the record, the Rusza-Szemerédi problem is [716]. That gives the correct formulation of the problem, so the formulation just needs to be fixed here as well. (Though once it is corrected, then it unfortunately becomes less applicable here.)
